report_text:
PRIVACY COMPLIANCE REPORT - REQ-001

Consumer: Maria Chen | State: California (CCPA/CPRA)
Request Type: Right to Know | Filed: 2026-01-10

SUMMARY: This report fulfills the consumer's right-to-know request under the California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA) as amended by the California Privacy Rights Act (CPRA). The consumer requested disclosure of all personal information collected, used, disclosed, or sold.

DATA SOURCES SEARCHED: Customer Master, Data Warehouse, Sales CRM. Three (3) matching records were identified across these sources with high-confidence match scores (4-5 out of 5).

PERSONAL INFORMATION DISCLOSED:
- Identifiers: Full name, email address, phone number, mailing address
- Commercial information: Vehicle purchase history, service records, financing details
- Internet activity: Website browsing history on dealer portal, the customer app app usage
- Geolocation: Dealer visit check-ins
- Inferences: Marketing segment classification, credit pre-qualification tier

THIRD-PARTY DISCLOSURES: Consumer data was shared with authorized dealer partners for service fulfillment and with credit bureaus for financing purposes.

DISPOSITION: All records disclosed to consumer per CCPA Section 1798.110. No exemptions applied. Report delivered via secure portal on 2026-02-01.
demo_scenario: Clear matches across 3 data sources — ideal happy-path walkthrough
Ambiguous match — consumer changed last name after marriage (Rodriguez to Chen). Demonstrates AI agent resolving identity with maiden name reasoning.

report_text:
PRIVACY COMPLIANCE REPORT - REQ-003

Consumer: James Williams | State: Connecticut (CTDPA)
Request Type: Opt-Out of Sale/Targeted Advertising | Filed: 2026-01-25

SUMMARY: This report confirms the consumer's opt-out request has been processed under the Connecticut Data Privacy Act (CTDPA). The consumer exercised their right to opt out of the sale of personal data and targeted advertising.

ACTIONS TAKEN:
- Marketing suppression flag applied across all data sources (Customer Master, Data Warehouse)
- Removed from targeted advertising segments and lookalike audiences
- Dealer partners notified to suppress marketing communications
- Third-party data sharing agreements updated to exclude this consumer
- Global Privacy Control (GPC) signal honored for future browser sessions

DATA RETAINED: Core transactional records retained for legal obligations (warranty, recall notifications, financial reporting). These records are exempt from opt-out under CTDPA Section 6(a).

VERIFICATION: Opt-out preferences propagated to 2 downstream vendors within 15 business days. Confirmation sent to consumer on 2026-02-15.
demo_scenario: Straightforward opt-out — clean processing with vendor notification
Ambiguous match — consumer's email domain changed (employer switch). Demonstrates AI reasoning about email domain differences.

report_text:
PRIVACY COMPLIANCE REPORT - REQ-005

Consumer: Emily Nakamura | State: California (CCPA/CPRA)
Request Type: Deletion | Filed: 2026-02-05

SUMMARY: This report confirms that a thorough search was conducted across all data sources in response to the consumer's deletion request under the CCPA/CPRA.

SEARCH RESULTS: Zero (0) matching records were found across all five data sources (Customer Master, Data Warehouse, Sales CRM, VehicleServices, DealerRecords). The search utilized name, email, phone, and state matching criteria.

CONCLUSION: As no personal information was found in our systems for this consumer, no deletion actions were required. The consumer has been notified that we do not hold personal information matching their provided identifiers.

NOTE: This finding is consistent with a consumer who has never interacted with our services or whose data was previously deleted under a prior request.
demo_scenario: Zero matches found — demonstrates the no-match path and appropriate consumer notification
Correction request with clear matches — consumer wants address updated across all records
High-volume consumer with many records across all data sources — tests pipeline performance with large result sets
Legal hold exception — deletion request blocked by active litigation hold on some records. Demonstrates exception handling in disposition.

report_text:
PRIVACY COMPLIANCE REPORT - REQ-009

Consumer: Karen Lee | State: Connecticut (CTDPA)
Request Type: Right to Know | Filed: 2026-02-15

SUMMARY: This report fulfills the consumer's right-to-know request under the Connecticut Data Privacy Act (CTDPA). Matching records were found in Customer Master and Sales CRM data sources.

DECODED DATA FIELDS: Several records contained coded demographic and behavioral fields. These have been decoded using the organization's cipher legend for full transparency:
- Income Code 'D' decoded to '$75,000-$99,999'
- Education Code '4' decoded to 'Bachelor's Degree'
- Occupation Code 'MGR' decoded to 'Management/Executive'
- Age Range Code '4' decoded to '35-44'
- Vehicle Segment Code 'SUV-M' decoded to 'Midsize SUV'
- Marketing Segment Code 'FAM-S' decoded to 'Family Suburban'

PERSONAL INFORMATION DISCLOSED:
- Identifiers: Full name, email, phone, mailing address
- Demographic inferences: Income range, education level, occupation category, age range, household composition
- Commercial data: Vehicle ownership history, service records
- Marketing classifications: Segment codes and targeting categories

DISPOSITION: All records disclosed with decoded field values per CTDPA requirements. Consumer notified via secure portal on 2026-03-01.
demo_scenario: Coded demographic fields — demonstrates the decode pipeline step where cipher codes are translated to human-readable values

Conflicting-law disposition — Texas right-to-delete (TDPSA) vs. federal CAN-SPAM evidence retention (16 CFR 316.5). Engine deletes consumer profile and CRM rows but retains the marketing-suppression record until 2029-11-10, with the federal citation in the audit trail.
Duplicate of REQ-008 — same consumer submitted again 2 days later
Duplicate of REQ-008 — same consumer with different email address, caught by phone+name matching
Duplicate of REQ-008 — nickname variant 'Mike' instead of 'Michael', caught by fuzzy name + email matching
Mixed disposition — some records full delete, some masked/anonymized, some retained under exemption. Demonstrates complex disposition logic.

Multi-jurisdictional household — requester (Thomas) is a California resident under CCPA; spouse and joint co-owner Linda is a Virginia resident under VCDPA. Each record gets its governing law applied independently: Thomas's sole-owner profile gets CCPA full-delete; the joint loyalty account and shared dealer purchase get VCDPA-aware mask-anonymize so Linda's data is preserved.

Namesake collision — two real, different consumers both named John Brown (one in TX, one in FL). Lexical name match returns both. The engine uses email + phone + state + address as disambiguating signals to exclude the FL John, prevents wrongful disclosure under TDPSA/CCPA, and records the exclusion explicitly in the audit trail.

Orphan VIN investigation — Mike Jackson right-to-know in Texas. Pipeline runs the new 2-step VIN lookup: name search → VIN expansion → VIN-keyed search bounded by the ownership window. In-window telematics, recall, manufacturing, and independent-shop records are pulled; out-of-window records (pre-Jane Smith, post-Bob Wilson) are excluded with explicit reasons. Separately, the Communication Coordinator surfaces orphan VIN JT4567890ABCDEFGH for investigation: drafts a Legacy CRM archive lookup (the customer-VIN link dropped during the 2019 CRM migration), parses the inbound reply attributing the VIN to Kenji Fukushima, and queues the attribution for operator approval. Same engine that handles consumer DSAR replies.

Consumer DSAR reply — Maria Chen replies to her CCPA deletion confirmation with a maiden-name claim ('I was previously known as Maria Salazar'). The Communication Coordinator parses the reply, classifies it as provides_new_identity_info, runs a deterministic candidate search under last_name=Salazar, and surfaces 3 new candidate records to the operator review queue. Operator must read the literal reply text before the Approve gate activates. On approve, the match search re-runs with the maiden-name attestation and 3 records get added to Maria's DSAR scope. Same Coordinator engine that handles orphan VIN investigation outreach — different recipient, different stakes.
